assertions.title: Forgot-password uses the canonical path and never relies on the retired alias

steps.expected: Login form displayed with forgot password link
steps.observed: Login form displayed with 'Forgot your password?' link visible
Confirmation shown: 'If an account with that address exists, we've sent a link to reset your password.'

steps.expected: Same confirmation message shown (email enumeration prevention)
steps.observed: Same confirmation message shown: 'If an account with that address exists...'

steps.action: Verify codebase does not call retired superadmin alias
steps.observed: authApi.ts explicitly documents the superadmin alias is retired (410 Gone) and only uses canonical /auth/password/forgot endpoint

evidence.network: POST /api/v1/auth/password/forgot (canonical path confirmed via code review)

assertions.title: Forgot-password controlled failures stay safe and retryable

steps.observed: Code correctly handles non-success ApiResponse by throwing ForgotPasswordPersistenceError with safe message

steps.expected: Persistence errors show retryable state; other errors show enum-safe confirmation
steps.observed: Persistence errors show 'Something went wrong' with 'Try again' button; other errors (network, 4xx, masked not-found) show the same confirmation message

steps.expected: Same confirmation message without revealing account existence
steps.observed: Same message 'If an account with that address exists...' shown for both existing and non-existent emails

evidence.network: No network errors observed during testing

assertions.title: Reset-password handles missing, invalid, expired, used, and superseded links safely

steps.observed: Redirected to http://127.0.0.1:3002/forgot-password (useEffect checks for missing token)
Form displayed with 'New password', 'Confirm password' fields and 'Reset password' button
Button remained [disabled] with weak password input

steps.expected: Button remains disabled, mismatch warning shown
steps.observed: Button remained [disabled], red border on confirm field, 'Passwords do not match' message shown

steps.action: Enter matching strong passwords and submit
steps.expected: Invalid token error with recovery messaging
steps.observed: Token error shown: 'Reset link invalid - This reset link is no longer valid. Please request a new one.' with 'Request a new reset link' button

evidence.network: POST /api/v1/auth/password/reset returned error for invalid token (expected)

summary: Tested 3 assertions: all passed. VAL-AUTH-011 confirmed canonical /forgot-password path with no usage of retired superadmin alias. VAL-AUTH-012 confirmed controlled failures show retryable state while other errors maintain email enumeration safety. VAL-AUTH-013 confirmed reset-password handles missing/invalid tokens with clear recovery messaging and password validation (weak passwords blocked, mismatched passwords blocked).
